findings.ref: tests/guard-r10-port.tests.ps1 (parity corpus) vs requirements.md Risks / design.md Test Strategy item 2
findings.note: AC-001 verification does not meet the spec's own stated bar. requirements.md Risks declares the Critical risk for this task as 'parity fixtures must exercise every protected suffix class, not a sample', and design.md mandates fixtures 'across every protected-suffix class'. The delivered corpus exercises only 7 protected-table entries. Zero parity coverage exists for: the ProtectedGatePluginJsonSuffixes set (a structurally distinct matching branch); the four review-loop agent .md files; kill-switch.{js,sh,ps1}; check-evidence-bundle.{sh,ps1,py}; validate_path.py; .claude/settings.local.json; three of four protected test files; task-review-loop SKILL.md; hooks.json and copilot-hooks.json; and the .ps1 guard's own self-protection entry. An incomplete port over an untested class would silently narrow Windows enforcement, which is exactly the failure mode the spec flags.

findings.ref: tests/guard-ps1-ascii.tests.sh (TARGET default / suite integration) vs AC-015, acceptance-tests.md TEST-015
findings.note: AC-015 requires the ASCII/no-BOM check to run 'in the test suite so a non-ASCII character introduced during the REQ-001 port cannot ship undetected'. The bundle contains no evidence that guard-ps1-ascii.tests.sh (or guard-r10-port.tests.ps1) is wired into tests/run-all.sh or the CI matrix. Additionally, the check's default TARGET is the staged human-copy file, not the live plugins/sdd-quality-loop/scripts path; without a GUARD_PS1 override the shipped live guard is not continuously covered after the staging copy is applied, weakening the AC's stated protection for Windows PowerShell 5.1 hosts.

Residual evasion in the ported cwd-aware analysis: the tokenizer returns $null on backslash escapes and the fallback substring pass fires only when the full protected path appears literally. This appears parity-faithful to the .py/.js twins and sits within the documented best-effort non-goal, but should be recorded as known residual risk.

The parity corpus drives all three twins only in exit-emit mode. The design architecture routes the Copilot hook path to the .ps1 twin in copilot-emit mode, but no scenario asserts the copilot JSON decision for the new R-10/forgery denials; decision logic is shared, so risk is low, but the emission path added by this port is unexercised in this task's evidence.
